reasoning: The 10-year Treasury yield has remained consistently well above 3.5% throughout 2025. In October 2025, it dipped below 4% to reach a 2025 low of roughly 3.97%, but as of late November 2025, it is hovering around 4.1%. Given that there is barely more than a month left in the year, a drop of roughly 60 basis points would require a sudden and severe catastrophic shock to the economy or uncharacteristically massive emergency rate cuts. The Polymarket consensus assigned a 3% probability to this event on Nov 13, and the passage of time without further downward movement makes it even less likely. Therefore, following the bounds allowed by the platform, I set the probability at the minimum threshold of 0.05.
